One mark each for any 3 of: • (cobalt / chloride) particles go from close together to spread out / (cobalt / chloride) particles go into solution / (cobalt / chloride) particles go into the water • diffusion • random movement of particles / particles move anywhere (in the liquid) / particles move in all directions • spreading out of particles / intermingling of particles / mixing of particles / particles collide / particles bounce off each other / particles go all over • (bulk) movement of particles from higher to lower concentration / movement of particles down concentration gradient to pink C O IF 2 marks not scored: 1 mark if all reversed / one consecutive pair reversed 0620/33 May/June 2018

3 of the following correct = 2 marks 1 or 2 of the following correct = 1 mark • Co has high melting point / boiling point ORA for lithium • Co has high density ORA for lithium • Co has catalytic activity ORA for lithium • Co forms coloured compounds ORA for lithium • Co compounds have variable oxidation states / form ions with different charges ORA • iron is hard / lithium is soft(er) / iron is strong / lithium is weak • one suitable difference in chemical properties e.g. cobalt is less reactive ORA / lithium reacts with cold water • cobalt is magnetic / ORA for lithium O loses oxygen / it loses oxygen / oxygen is removed from the cobalt oxide / the cobalt oxide loses oxygen 0620/33 May/June 2018 C=C double bond / red-brown / brown to colourless –120 ºC is in between the melting point and boiling point / –120 ºC is higher than the melting point AND lower than the boiling point correct structure of ethanol showing all atoms and all bonds ºC enzymes distillation any suitable use e.g. solvent / to make named chemical energy level of reactants above energy level of products / the arrow is going downwards / energy (level) goes down / product has less energy than reactants carbon 0620/33 May/June 2018

questions.text: 5 (a) Molten lead(II) bromide can be electrolysed using the apparatus shown. molten lead(II) bromide inert electrodes +– The negative electrode is called the cathode. State the name of the positive electrode. .............................................................................................................................................. [1] (b) Predict the products of t

parts.answer: negative electrode: lead positive electrode: bromine

add (sufficient) water and stir (to dissolve) / add the mixture to water filter off the lead bromide / filter to obtain solution sodium bromide evaporate (some of the) water from the solution / warm solution to crystallisation point then leave (to crystallise) chlorine is more reactive than bromine 0620/33 May/June 2018

2nd box down ticked 4th box down ticked

number of protons plus neutrons in an atom 0620/33 May/June 2018 relative hardness of potassium: between 1 and 3 (exclusive of these two values) melting point of rubidium: less than 63 ºC but not lower than 20 ºC / fewer bubbles than sodium Group I hydroxide is alkaline / sodium hydroxide is an alkali add (red) litmus OR add universal / full range indicator turns blue OR turns blue / purple IF two marks not scored: 1 mark for (B =) 11, (Na =) 23, (H =) 1 0620/33 May/June 2018
